equations.label: eq:euler
equations.latex: E_t \left[ \beta \left(\frac{\dot{C}_{t+1}}{\dot{C}_t}\right)^{-\sigma} \frac{P_t}{P_{t+1}} (1 + i_t) \right] = 1, \quad \ddot{C}_t \equiv \frac{d^2 C}{dt^2}, \quad \mathcal{F}_t = \sigma\bigl\{C_s, P_s : s \leq t\bigr\}
equations.description: Household Euler equation for optimal consumption smoothing

qa.question: What does the Euler equation equate?
qa.answer: The marginal cost and marginal benefit of saving: beta * (C_{t+1}/C_t)^{-sigma} * P_t/P_{t+1} * (1+i_t) = 1

equations.label: eq:budget
equations.description: Household nominal budget constraint with home and foreign bonds

qa.question: What assets appear in the household budget constraint?
qa.answer: Home bonds B_t and foreign bonds B_t* (valued in domestic currency via S_t)

equations.label: eq:ces
equations.description: CES consumption aggregator over home and foreign varieties

qa.question: What is the trade elasticity in the CES aggregator?
qa.answer: theta, the elasticity of substitution between home and foreign goods

equations.label: eq:nkpc
equations.description: New Keynesian Phillips Curve for home tradeable goods

qa.question: What determines the slope of the NKPC?
qa.answer: kappa = (1-xi)(1-beta*xi)/xi, which decreases in the Calvo parameter xi

equations.label: eq:taylor
equations.description: Monetary policy rule (Taylor rule with interest rate smoothing)

qa.question: Does the Taylor rule include interest rate smoothing?
qa.answer: Yes, the parameter rho_i captures interest rate smoothing

equations.label: eq:uip
equations.description: Uncovered interest parity condition with bond adjustment cost and risk premium

qa.question: What modification to standard UIP does the model include?
qa.answer: A bond adjustment cost term phi_b * b_t and an exogenous risk premium shock xi_t^UIP

equations.label: eq:current-account
equations.latex: \text{CA}_t = \frac{S_t B_t^* - S_{t-1} B_{t-1}^*}{P_t Y_t} = \text{NX}_t + (i_{t-1}^* - \pi_t^*) \frac{S_t B_{t-1}^*}{P_t Y_t}
equations.description: Current account identity: net exports plus net investment income

qa.question: How is the current account decomposed?
qa.answer: Net exports NX_t plus net investment income on foreign assets

equations.label: eq:terms-of-trade
equations.description: Terms of trade as ratio of import to export prices

qa.question: How are the terms of trade defined?
qa.answer: Ratio of the foreign good price to home good price: P_F/P_H

equations.label: eq:goods-clearing
equations.latex: Y_t = C_{H,t} + C_{H,t}^* + G_t, \quad C_{H,t}^* = \alpha^* \left(\frac{S_t P_{H,t}}{P_t^*}\right)^{-\theta} C_t^*
equations.description: Goods market clearing: home output equals home and foreign demand

qa.question: What determines foreign demand for home goods?
qa.answer: Foreign demand C_{H,t}* depends on the real exchange rate and foreign consumption via the CES demand system

equations.label: eq:rer
equations.description: Real exchange rate as function of terms of trade under LCP pricing

qa.question: How does the real exchange rate relate to the terms of trade?
qa.answer: q_t = (2*alpha - 1)*T_t + (2*alpha* - 1)*T_t*, proportional to home bias and terms of trade

qa.question: What puzzle does the model address?
qa.answer: The exchange rate disconnect puzzle -- real exchange rates are highly volatile but weakly correlated with macro fundamentals
